title: Question: 64-bit function return to a scalar?
body:
---


Author Name: **Elliot Mednick**
Original Redmine Message: 1995 from https://www.veripool.org



---

The following example generates a c++ error at compile time:
```
reg [63:0] Reg_R[0:15];
...
    export "DPI-C" function tb_reg_read; 
    function [63:0] tb_reg_read
       (
         input int addr
        );
       tb_reg_read = Reg_R[addr];
    endfunction

The error is:
Vfe.cpp: In static member function 'static svBitVecVal Vfe::tb_reg_read(int)':
Vfe.cpp:124:12: error: invalid conversion from 'svBitVecVal* {aka unsigned int*}' to 'svBitVecVal {aka unsigned int}' [-fpermissive]
      return tb_reg_read__Vfuncrtn;
             ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
Vfe.cpp:122:17: warning: address of local variable 'tb_reg_read__Vfuncrtn' returned [-Wreturn-local-addr]
      svBitVecVal tb_reg_read__Vfuncrtn [2];
 
```
It looks like it doesn't want to return a 64-bit integer, and wants two 32-bit vectors.  I tried --pin64.
Latest version of Verilator.  Is it me?

Thanks.
